message:
fix: replace mutex .unwrap()/.expect() with poison-resilient recovery

Systematically replace all .lock().unwrap() and .lock().expect()
calls with .lock().unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner) across
test helper code in four modules:

- extension_dispatcher.rs: 30+ test Mutex accesses (TestSession,
  CaptureUiHandler, test assertions) now recover from poison
- extensions.rs: CaptureLayer snapshot and ExtensionManager test
  init now poison-resilient
- hostcall_queue.rs: 6 concurrent test Mutex accesses for epoch
  pin and multi-producer tests
- sdk.rs: 17 listener/event test Mutex accesses including
  SessionOptions on_event callback and subscribe/unsubscribe
- tui.rs: buffer lock in markdown rendering test

This prevents test panics from cascading through poisoned mutexes
in the multi-agent concurrent test environment where one test
thread's panic could previously poison shared state and cause
unrelated test failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
